title: Implement CSV renderer for issue-linked benchmark data
description:
Add a dedicated CSV renderer for issue-linked benchmark and scorecard artifacts in the Web UI.

## Background

The agreed v1 behavior for `.csv` documents is a sortable table with a sticky header, inline rich/raw switching, search highlighting, and capped rich preview behavior for large files. The referenced `../gf2` issues include benchmark CSVs that are easier to inspect in tabular form than as raw text.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `.csv` documents render in a dedicated rich view that shows parsed rows and columns instead of raw text by default.
- [ ] The rich CSV view supports sortable columns and a sticky header.
- [ ] The CSV renderer supports inline switching between rich and raw views.
- [ ] Search queries highlight matching CSV cells in the rich view.
- [ ] Large CSV files cap the rich preview and keep the full content available through the raw view.
- [ ] Renderer-specific tests cover CSV parsing, table rendering, sorting, search highlighting, and capped-preview behavior.

## Notes

Use a small CSV parsing dependency for v1 rather than a narrow custom parser.
